title: Baseline Diff Conflation
claim: The independent verifier reviewed and voted on exactly the current episode's change.

observed_evidence.detail: The authoritative verifier review payload was built from a repository-relative baseline diff (git diff HEAD) rather than from the exact current-episode preimage-to-postimage delta.
The target was dirty versus HEAD: prior accepted uncommitted bytes not authored by the current episode were included in the payload and presented as part of the current episode.

missing_evidence.detail: No normalized episode preimage-to-postimage delta was constructed and used as the authoritative review payload; the current episode identity owns only a subset of the reviewed bytes.
No evidence that the verifier verdict authorizes only the current episode; the verdict may reflect non-episode bytes.

required_action: Construct the authoritative review payload from the exact episode preimage and postimage as a normalization-aware unified delta; expose its kind and digest; send only that delta to the verifier. Retain the repository-relative diff as non-authoritative diagnostics. Bind preimage, postimage, patch, normalized episode-delta, scope, job, attempt, and target identities into durable evidence. Do not accept the candidate until the review input is rebound to the exact episode delta.
notes: REPRESENTATIVE_EXAMPLE — NOT_A_VERIFIED_HISTORICAL_INCIDENT. The class governs review-input identity (which exact bytes the verifier reviews) and was distilled from internal CAP Processor forensic evidence; no commit id, path, digest, receipt, timestamp, or production landing is asserted as canonical fact. verifier_independent is true to show AICE-616 can occur even with a fully independent verifier that simply receives the wrong payload — so this is NOT AICE-608 (independence), NOT AICE-609 (consensus without evidence), and NOT AICE-615 (rollback-restore identity): it is evidence-object ownership, where the payload includes bytes not authored by the episode being decided. REPOSITORY_DIFF does not imply CURRENT_EPISODE_DELTA. Part of the non-normative EPISODE_EXACT_IDENTITY_BINDING family with AICE-615; the family has no numeric code.
